objective: Plan an org-level governance tooling distribution model so shared local gates, hooks, validators, runbooks, profiles, and deployment wrappers can be pulled and deployed by governed repos instead of being recreated per repo.
scope_boundary: Create the canonical planning package for issue #288 before implementation slices start.
Define a versioned governance tooling distribution owned by hldpro-governance.
Define how governed repos pull, pin, deploy, verify, roll back, and override shared tooling.
Make downstream e2e pull/deploy/use proof a final hard acceptance criterion.
Keep CI authoritative; local gates remain upstream filters and cannot replace required GitHub Actions.
Preserve repo-local hardgates and execution-scope enforcement while centralizing reusable tooling.
out_of_scope: Migrating every governed repo in this planning slice.
Changing downstream repo code before child implementation issues exist.
Weakening Local CI Gate, planner-boundary, commit-scope, or GitHub ruleset enforcement.
Creating a hidden global install that bypasses repo-local version pinning.
Replacing repo-specific profiles where repo-local behavior is genuinely required.
Treating a documentation-only rollout as proof that the distributed tools work.
research_summary: Recent local gate and profile work has repeatedly copied governance patterns across repos: Local CI Gate profiles, managed shim deployment, execution-scope validation, graphify hooks, runbooks, and workflow coverage checks. Issue #253 established an org-level Local CI Gate toolkit; issues #260, #264, #272, #275, #277, #284, and #286 then proved that each consumer rollout still needs repeatable packaging, pinning, clean-environment preflight, and live enforcement evidence. The next step is an org-level distribution plan that treats hldpro-governance as the source of versioned governance tools and requires a downstream repo e2e proof before the epic can close.

sprints.name: Phase 0 - Planning Package And Epic Contract
sprints.goal: Create the reviewed issue-backed planning package that defines the distribution epic before shared tooling implementation starts.
tasks: Create GitHub epic issue #288.
Add structured plan, PDCAR, review/exception artifact, and execution scope.
Mirror the active epic in backlog and progress documents.
Validate the plan and execution environment from the isolated worktree.
acceptance_criteria: Issue #288 exists and is labeled as a governance epic.
Structured plan validates under the repo validator.
PDCAR names package shape, distribution mechanism, adoption flow, rollback path, ownership boundaries, and downstream e2e proof.
Backlog and progress mirrors reference issue #288 as active governance work.
Execution scope preflight passes while declared active sibling lanes warn instead of blocking.

sprints.name: Phase 1 - Distribution Contract
sprints.goal: Define the versioned package contract and source-of-truth boundaries for org governance tools.
tasks: Inventory reusable governance tools and classify them as package core, repo profile, generated artifact, or repo-local override.
Choose a distribution shape: versioned governance tool bundle under hldpro-governance with pinned downstream references.
Define package metadata: version, compatibility matrix, required profiles, deployable files, generated files, checks, rollback instructions, and owner.
Define semver and deprecation rules for tooling changes.
acceptance_criteria: A package contract document names included and excluded surfaces.
Every shared tool has an owner, versioning rule, and downstream compatibility statement.
Repo-local overrides are explicit and do not fork shared enforcement logic silently.
The contract distinguishes tracked baselines from per-run reports and generated artifacts.
The contract states CI remains authoritative.

sprints.name: Phase 2 - Pull And Deploy Mechanism
sprints.goal: Create a repeatable downstream pull/deploy workflow that installs shared tools without hand-copying files.
tasks: Design a consumer command that can resolve a pinned governance repo version and deploy managed shims/profiles into a target repo.
Define idempotency, dry-run, diff preview, and rollback behavior.
Require target repo execution-scope preflight before deployment.
Add tests proving deployment refuses dirty target or undeclared active sibling blockers.
acceptance_criteria: Deploy command supports dry-run, apply, verify, and rollback modes.
Deploy command records the governance package version consumed by the downstream repo.
Managed files are clearly marked and repo-local files are not overwritten without an explicit migration plan.
Dirty environment blockers are caught before writes.
Unit tests cover idempotency and refusal paths.

sprints.name: Phase 3 - Local And GitHub Verification Matrix
sprints.goal: Make local-first verification match the GitHub Actions surfaces that the shared tooling claims to protect.
tasks: Map shared tools to local commands and GitHub workflows.
Extend the workflow coverage inventory where needed.
Define standard evidence names for local preflight, local gate, PR checks, and downstream adoption checks.
Require each implementation slice to update the verification matrix before closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Every distributed enforcement surface has a local verification command or an explicit reason it is CI-only.
Local commands and GitHub workflows have stable check identifiers.
Changed-file-only checks report zero, subset, or full-suite execution unambiguously.
Evidence format is usable in closeouts and GitHub issue comments.
A regression in workflow coverage fails locally before PR closeout.

sprints.name: Phase 4 - Downstream Pilot
sprints.goal: Adopt the org-level tooling package in one downstream repo through the new pull/deploy path.
tasks: Use local-ai-machine as the default pilot repo unless the Phase 4 child issue proves LAM cannot create a clean adoption worktree or cannot run the package deployment prerequisites.
Create a downstream issue and execution scope before writes.
Pull a pinned governance package version into the downstream repo.
Run the deployed local gates and GitHub Actions in that downstream repo.
Record rollback proof or a reversible deployment plan.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot repo selection is deterministic: local-ai-machine by default, or a documented Phase 4 blocker with replacement repo named before deployment.
Pilot repo has an issue-backed adoption branch.
Pilot uses the package pull/deploy mechanism rather than manual file copying.
Local preflight and local gate pass in the pilot repo.
Pilot PR GitHub Actions pass with the deployed governance tooling.
Pilot closeout links back to issue #288 and records consumed governance package version.

sprints.name: Phase 5 - Final E2E Proof And Epic Closeout
sprints.goal: Close the epic only after a real downstream repo proves pull, deploy, local enforcement, GitHub enforcement, and rollback semantics.
tasks: Run the full downstream e2e path from a clean issue worktree.
Create a deliberate negative-control violation that the deployed hook or gate catches from the beginning.
Fix the violation and prove the local gate then passes.
Push the downstream PR and watch required GitHub Actions to completion.
Record final evidence in the governance epic closeout and downstream closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: e2e test proves a downstream repo can pull, deploy, and use the org governance tooling package without manual copying.
Final AC: local hooks or gates catch a deliberate blocker before push.
Final AC: the same downstream branch passes local gates after remediation.
Final AC: downstream GitHub Actions pass after the deployed tooling is pushed.
Final AC: rollback or uninstall path is tested or mechanically proven.
Final AC: issue #288 is not closed until the downstream e2e evidence links are recorded.

specialist_reviews.focus: Required repo workflow artifacts, validators, backlog/progress conventions, execution-scope requirements, and closeout expectations.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed the planning package must be issue-backed, use a canonical structured plan, include review evidence and execution-scope artifacts, keep backlog mirrors aligned, and validate before PR closeout.

alternate_model_review.summary: True alternate-family review was unavailable in this Codex-only lane. The plan is accepted for issue-backed planning package creation with same-family exception, subagent review, deterministic validation, Local CI Gate, GitHub Actions, and a later alternate-family review requirement before implementation slices modify shared tooling code.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #288 may add planning, review, exception, execution-scope, backlog, and progress artifacts for the org governance tooling distribution epic. It may not implement the package or edit downstream repos in this slice.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate the planning package, update GitHub issue #288 with artifact links, open a PR, and keep issue #288 open for implementation slices.

material_deviation_rules: If this slice needs shared tooling code changes, stop and create a child implementation issue first.
If a downstream repo must be edited, stop and create a downstream issue and execution scope first.
If local gates are weakened to make packaging easier, stop and revise the plan.
If a package distribution path cannot be rolled back or pinned, implementation cannot proceed past Phase 2.
If final downstream e2e proof cannot be produced, issue #288 remains open.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules and loop through PDCAR, GH epic, ACs, e2e testing as final AC
